From that singular point, everything moved away from each other as space itself grew. Scientists classify this enduring movement as cosmic expansion. During this era, radiation cooled sufficiently for protons and electrons to combine. Eventually, gravity pulled gas clouds together to ignite stars. Observations of light from distant objects confirm that structures continue to drift apart across the void.<|im_end|>
